content:
# Porosity

**Summary**: The measure of void spaces in a rock.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2002-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2002]] modeled the spatial distribution of porosity using seismic and well data.

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] Not used in this thesis.

- [[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]] Not applicable to this thesis.

- [[2001-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2001]] analyzed porosity as a primary reservoir property in the Hanifa Reservoir.

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] measured and estimated porosity using helium porosimetry and petrographic point-counting.

- [[1997-al-marhoun|Al-Marhoun, 1997]] quantified porosity using both well logs and seismic attributes.

- [[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993]] evaluated the storage capacities and development of aquifers as vital issues for Somalia.

- [[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000]] measured porosity to correlate with acoustic and electrical properties.

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] analyzed porosity as a primary physical property for geostatistical modeling.

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] evaluated how diagenesis affects porosity in the Jauf sandstone.

- [[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996]] analyzed the spatial distribution of well-log porosity.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] evaluated porosity as a key indicator of reservoir quality.

- [[2003-al-shridi|Al-Shridi, 2003]] analyzed porosity as a primary and secondary component for reservoir characterization.

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] Not applicable

- [[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009]] measured porosity to evaluate the accuracy of the neural network predictions.

- [[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010]] measured porosity to determine the volume available for fluids and define petrophysical rock types.

- [[2011-salamah|Salamah, 2011]] Not used in this thesis.

- [[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]] not used in this study

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] mapped the distribution of porosity in the sequence stratigraphic cross-section.

- [[2012-amao|Amao, 2012]] Not used directly as a primary measurement, but implied through the analysis of boring tunnels.

- [[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012]] analyzed how porosity is controlled by fractures rather than the matrix.

- [[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]] Not used in this thesis.

- [[2013-yousif|Yousif, 2013]] measured porosity across three sampling sections to build a 3D model.

- [[2013-abdullah|Abdullah, 2013]] measured porosity to compare standard and LSA results.

- [[2013-el-kheir|El-Kheir, 2013]] calculated porosity using sonic, density, and neutron logs.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] measured porosity across various lithofacies to characterize the reservoir.

- [[2013-mohammed|Mohammed, 2013]] Not used in this study.

- [[2014-kola|Kola, 2014]] not used directly as a primary measurement but implied in reservoir characterization.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] measured porosity across three zones of the Al-Ilb paleochannel to assess reservoir quality.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] measured porosity to evaluate the impact of texture and diagenesis on reservoir quality.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] analyzed porosity to characterize the reservoir quality of the Khartam Member.

- [[2014-saeed|Saeed, 2014]] quantified porosity as a key petrophysical parameter for cavity-filling materials.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] measured porosity to assess reservoir quality.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] measured porosity in the Siq Sandstone.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] measured porosity to evaluate the storage capacity of the Qusaiba Shale.

- [[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015]] calculated porosity using both well logs and predicted values from seismic attributes.

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] measured porosity to assess the impact of activation and chemical treatment on carbon structure.

- [[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015]] calculated various types of porosity to determine the average value for modeling.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] measured porosity to evaluate the reservoir quality of the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] evaluated porosity to assess reservoir quality in the Otu field.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] measured porosity across various lithofacies to assess reservoir potential.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] measured porosity across different lithofacies to assess reservoir quality.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] measured porosity to characterize the reservoir quality of the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] measured porosity types including intergranular, moldic, and intragranular.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] measured porosity across Khuff A, B, and C units to evaluate reservoir quality.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] measured porosity evolution as a function of diagenetic processes.

- [[2016-ali|Ali, 2016]] tested the inversion method on synthetic traces representing the Arab formation across a range of porosities (10%, 20%, and 30%).

- [[2016-houssain|Houssain, 2016]] calculated porosity from conditioned logs and predicted it using the stiff-sand model.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] measured porosity from core plug samples.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] measured porosity and permeability to assess reservoir quality.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] measured porosity to evaluate the reservoir quality and heterogeneity.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] measured porosity to evaluate the impact of diagenesis on reservoir quality.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] measured porosity to assess the reservoir quality of the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] measured porosity to evaluate the impact of diagenesis and grain size on reservoir quality.

- [[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018]] analyzed the mesoporosity and microporosity of SDAC using N2 adsorption isotherms.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] measured porosity to evaluate the reservoir quality of different microfacies.

- [[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019]] predicted porosity and gas saturation zones.

- [[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020]] used porosity as a key factor in determining how much CO2 can be stored in a site.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] measured porosity across different lithofacies in the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] used porosity as a primary volumetric property to be derived from seismic data.

- [[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020]] analyzed total and connected porosity using Micro-CT scans.

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] Not stated in source

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] measured porosity across several Jurassic carbonate formations.

- [[2021-ahmed|Ahmed, 2021]] measured porosity using helium expansion and analyzed its distribution across different pore types.

- [[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021]] Not directly measured but implied through mineralogy and log analysis.

- [[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021]] measured porosity to evaluate the impact of early cementation and compaction on reservoir quality.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] measured porosity to evaluate the petrophysical properties of the core samples.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] measured porosity in the mudstones to characterize reservoir quality.

- [[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022]] measured porosity to evaluate the impact of pore systems on reservoir quality.

- [[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]] Not used as a primary measurement in this study.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] evaluated the relationship between porosity and sonic velocity across multiple carbonate formations.

- [[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]] modeled the porosity distribution using geostatistical methods.

- [[2023-kusuma|Kusuma, 2023]] measured porosity across 1D, 2D, and 3D scales using various ML models.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] Not used in this study.

- [[2023-aldhafeeri|Aldhafeeri, 2023]] measured porosity changes after wormhole generation.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] used porosity as a key parameter in both Gassmann and Hertz-Mindlin models.

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] evaluated the impact of mineralogy and kerogen on porosity estimation.

- [[2024-tarchi|Tarchi, 2024]] eliminated the dependence on porosity during inversion.

- [[2024-alkhunaizi|Alkhunaizi, 2024]] used porosity as a variable in the VG (phi, SW) relationship for GPR data.

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] measured porosity across the Qusaiba and Sharawra members to assess reservoir quality.

- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] measured porosity to evaluate the impact of burrow filling and size.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] studied the porosity patterns of these carbonates qualitatively and quantitatively.

- [[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]] measured porosity across different stromatolite forms.

- [[2024-akomolafe|Akomolafe, 2024]] evaluated porosity using NMR T2 distribution analysis.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] measured porosity to correlate with acoustic velocity across different bioturbation styles.

- [[2025-oyekan|Oyekan, 2025]] analyzed porosity to determine its impact on the inversion of water saturation.

- [[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025]] Not used in this thesis.

- [[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025]] measured porosity to determine the impact of burrow fillings on reservoir quality.

- [[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025]] measured porosity within the Minjur Sandstone.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] measured porosity to evaluate the impact of CO2 on the pore structure of carbonate mudrocks.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] quantified porosity to evaluate the reservoir quality of the Arab-D member.

- [[1987-abdul-latif|Abdul-Latif, 1987]] Not stated in source.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] evaluated the relationship between porosity and mechanical properties.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2002-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2002]] Measured via wireline logs and core analysis.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Porosity values are critical for determining flow units; high-permeability zones have >14% intergranular porosity. ([[2002-ibrahim|Ibrahim, 2002, p. 18]])

- Not stated in source ([[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]])

- Not stated in source ([[1993-kaka|Kaka, 1993]])

- Porosity is essential for development drilling, reserve estimation, and fluid flow simulation. ([[2001-al-khalifa|Al-Khalifa, 2001, p. 25]])

- Porosity was used as a primary input for calculating hydraulic conductivity and transmissivity. ([[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995, p. 53]])

- Porosity was estimated using multi-variant regression from nine different seismic attributes. ([[1997-al-marhoun|Al-Marhoun, 1997, p. 16]])

- Storage coefficients were calculated for several aquifers to determine their ability to supply water. ([[1993-moallim|Moallim, 1993, p. 66]])

- The Khobar aquifer generally has a porosity of more than 3%. ([[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000, p. 30]])

- Statistical analysis of the physical properties of SFNC Sequence such as porosity and facies based porosity was done. ([[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997, p. 16]])

- Porosity can be enhanced or reduced by diagenetic processes, including compaction, cementation, and dissolution. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 20]])

- Well-log derived porosity values from 44 vertical wells were used to construct geostatistical models. ([[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996, p. 19]])

- Secondary porosity from dissolution is an important diagenetic event for the formation. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 23]])

- Primary porosity is determined by texture; secondary porosity in carbonates results from dissolution, recrystallization, and dolomitization. ([[2003-al-shridi|Al-Shridi, 2003, p. 27]])

- Not stated in source ([[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]])

- Porosity is a key factor for reservoir modeling and was predicted using both MLP and GRNN models. ([[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009, p. 15]])

- Total porosity measured by density or neutron logs includes micro, small, large-connected, and isolated vugs. ([[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010, p. 106]])

- Not stated in source ([[2011-salamah|Salamah, 2011]])

- Not stated in source ([[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]])

- Muddy stromatolites were found to have low porosity compared to ooid grainstones. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 51]])

- Not stated in source ([[2012-amao|Amao, 2012]])

- Porosity and permeability in basement reservoirs are controlled by the fracture network. ([[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012, p. 11]])

- Not stated in source ([[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]])

- Porosity values ranged from 0% to 40% across the sampled lithofacies. ([[2013-yousif|Yousif, 2013, p. 34]])

- Standard methods underestimate porosity in thin-bedded formations due to the averaging effect of low-resolution tools. ([[2013-abdullah|Abdullah, 2013, p. 16]])

- Porosity distribution maps were generated for the Aradeiba-D sand zone. ([[2013-el-kheir|El-Kheir, 2013, p. 41]])

- Porosity values were assigned based on correlation between outcrop and subsurface data from three fields. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 40]])

- Not stated in source ([[2013-mohammed|Mohammed, 2013]])

- Not stated in source ([[2014-kola|Kola, 2014]])

- Porosity values for the proximal part are between 15% and 25%, with an average of 20%. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 126]])

- Porosity was preserved by authigenic kaolinite coatings preventing quartz overgrowth. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 16]])

- Porosity types included moldic, vuggy, and intercrystalline, with different distributions based on pore size. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 74]])

- The joint inversion method accurately retrieved porosity values within 2.5% error. ([[2014-saeed|Saeed, 2014, p. 17]])

- Grainstone units in KM-1 reached 33% porosity, while KM-2 peaked at 34%. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 80]])

- Porosity was calculated to be as high as 25% in some parts of the Siq Sandstone. ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014, p. 83]])

- Interparticle porosity in Lithofacies-I is very low (<1%). ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 40]])

- RBF models successfully predicted porosity with low validation errors across multiple wells. ([[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015, p. 76]])

- AC-HNO3-90°C had the highest pore volume (0.76 cm3/g) among all samples. ([[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015, p. 110]])

- Average porosity showed a good correlation with core porosity (R2 = 0.85 and 0.79). ([[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015, p. 77]])

- Maximum porosity values reached 32-25% in specific sand shoal grainstone facies. ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015, p. 13]])

- Average effective porosity was calculated for various reservoirs (Table 3.3). ([[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015, p. 100]])

- Porosity values ranged from 2% (Bpp) to 30% (Csg and Cbg). ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 48]])

- Porosity was significantly reduced in LF1 due to calcite cementation and in LF9 due to mica content. ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 14]])

- Porosity-permeability relationship exhibits poor correlation for these lithofacies. ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016, p. 13]])

- Several facies showed high visual porosity (e.g., Gp at 15%, Gl at 25%). ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 107]])

- Porosity in Khuff B dolostones reached up to 23.2% in crystalline samples. ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 53]])

- Meteoric phreatic diagenesis led to great enhancement of the original porosity across all lithofacies. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 96]])

- The inversion of these cases also results in similarly small error between the inverted and true fluid properties for different porosity levels. ([[2016-ali|Ali, 2016, p. 14]])

- The stiff-sand model provides equations to estimate porosity from seismically derived impedance. ([[2016-houssain|Houssain, 2016, p. 17]])

- Porosity values were distributed across different lithofacies and rock types (grainy, muddy-grainy, muddy). ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 60]])

- Porosity values were correlated with TOC and acoustic impedance for prediction. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 163]])

- Porosity in Well-A ranged from 4.5% to 13.5%. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 140]])

- Porosity values ranged from less than 2% to a maximum of 19%. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 21]])

- Porosity values were used to calculate RQI and FZI for flow unit identification. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 71]])

- Average porosity in sandstones (Facies A and B) ranges from 15-20%. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 199]])

- All adsorbents (except 2Z-500-30) were found to be predominantly mesoporous in nature. ([[2018-adeyemi|Adeyemi, 2018, p. 64]])

- Porosity was reduced by micritization and cementation in mudstone facies. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 17]])

- Porosity volume estimation was compared between real and synthetic well inversions. ([[2019-al-dawood|Al-Dawood, 2019, p. 80]])

- Porosity values were predicted from well log data using an ANN with 18 neurons and a tan-sigmoid transfer function. ([[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020, p. 17]])

- Porosity values were correlated with specific diagenetic features like dissolution and micritization. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 109]])

- The reservoir rocks have total porosity not exceeding 15%. ([[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020, p. 18]])

- Western side has higher connectivity and larger pores compared to the eastern side. ([[2020-mansouri|Mansouri, 2020, p. 72]])

- Not stated in source ([[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]])

- Coefficient of variation (CV) for porosity ranged between 0.261 and 0.590. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 13]])

- Vuggy and moldic pores were the primary contributors to macro-porosity in the upper parts. ([[2021-ahmed|Ahmed, 2021, p. 67]])

- Not stated in source ([[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021]])

- Porosity ranges from 0-6.7% with an average of 0.8%. ([[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021, p. 142]])

- Measured core samples porosity ranged from 31.6% to 42.92% with an average of 39%. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 124]])

- Average total porosity in the J1 unit is 7.4%. ([[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022, p. 51]])

- Porosity was calculated by subtracting the grain volume from its bulk volume and the volume determined by helium injection. ([[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022, p. 50]])

- Not stated in source ([[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]])

- Porosity showed an inverse relationship with both compressional (Vp) and shear (Vs) velocities. ([[2022-salih|Salih, 2022, p. 13]])

- Grain-dominated facies (LFA-1, LFA-2, and LFA-3) exhibit better petrophysical properties including higher porosity than mud-dominated facies. ([[2022-babker|Babker, 2022, p. 84]])

- ANN reached an R2 of 0.95 for predicting missing log porosity. ([[2023-kusuma|Kusuma, 2023, p. 17]])

- Not stated in source ([[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]])

- Porosity increased significantly due to the creation of wormholes. ([[2023-aldhafeeri|Aldhafeeri, 2023, p. 14]])

- Used to calculate the dry state properties of the sediment. ([[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023, p. 27]])

- Porosity was successfully estimated from seismic-scale elastic volumes using both carbonate and mudrock models. ([[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024, p. 123]])

- A constant porosity of 0.35 was used only for converting Sw to water content (WC) for comparison purposes. ([[2024-tarchi|Tarchi, 2024, p. 65]])

- Porosity was used as a known parameter to isolate the effect of water saturation on GPR velocity. ([[2024-alkhunaizi|Alkhunaizi, 2024, p. 12]])

- Porosity values ranged from 2.37% to 37.58%. ([[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024, p. 15]])

- Different types of porosity (channel, vug, moldic) were identified in the matrix. ([[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024, p. 44]])

- Mudstones, mud-supported packstones, and wackestone have low porosity (about 2.5%). ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 36]])

- Porosity values were relatively low and consistent between CF (33.9-48%) and LL (32.3-54.9%). ([[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024, p. 17]])

- Inaccurate assessment of porosity occurs when the bulk relaxation of drilling fluid is not accounted for. ([[2024-akomolafe|Akomolafe, 2024, p. 12]])

- Porosity values for both BN and BR styles were comparable, despite differences in pore shape and connectivity. ([[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024, p. 63]])

- Tested porosity values ranged from 25% to a critical value of 45% at 1% intervals for sensitivity analysis. ([[2025-oyekan|Oyekan, 2025, p. 69]])

- Not stated in source ([[2025-rachmadan|Rachmadan, 2025]])

- Porosity was significantly higher in pipe-framework intervals (up to 7.75%) compared to bio-retexturing intervals (1.64% - 2.71%). ([[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025, p. 60]])

- Porosity values for sandstones typically range between 10 and 20 percent. ([[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025, p. 25]])

- Porosity changes were observed following CO2 treatment, influenced by mineral precipitation and dissolution. ([[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025, p. 40]])

- Unit 2A has an average porosity over 25%, while Unit 3B averages only 5%. ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 108]])
